concept: Wondrous / Wonderful / Exceeding What Was Looked For
description: Wondrous: exceeding what was looked for, and lifting the looker as it passes. A summer that outgrows every plan for it, a change no story prepared, a morning that makes the sleeper sit up. Where 'phelora' (beautiful) names what satisfies the eye and 'horae' (radiant) what shines outward, 'waora' names what overflows the expectation itself: the quality in things that makes a person say 'wao'.
sound_symbolism: The word begins with the complete exclamation 'wao' (wow), the same caught-open breath taught to stand still and describe. Final 'ra' lifts that response outward and lets the wonder continue beyond the first instant of surprise.
grammatical_notes: Adjective. 'ha keloi waora to nai.' (That summer was wondrous.) 'whekelu ru kuewa nela ru waora nai.' (Magic is very strange and very wondrous.) Kin to 'wao' (wow), the response it earns, and 'wakomi' (surprise), the arrival it often rides in on. The quality noun, wonder, comes by rule.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Wonder is attention caught fully awake; a word for it keeps the practice of being astonishable.
pillars.art-nouveau-aesthetics: The movement built whole doorways to say this word: the ordinary vine, drawn until it exceeds itself.

semantic_domains.cognition: What outruns what was looked for
